meaning: adj.标准以下的,不合规格的
examples: It comes as a shock to us that some milk powder producers produce substandard milk powder which would bring harm to babies.
The cold has made already substandard living conditions even worse.
Residents in general are poor and undereducated, and live in substandard housing.
In Hong Kong, such loans are classified as "substandard" even if the debtor is still repaying.
Forcing such substandard employees to commute to work does not change their character, or lack thereof.
